headline: LCS Cost RM11.22 Billion; Maharaja Lela First Sea-Going Jan 2026, Programme Cut 6 to 5
context: The Littoral Combat Ship contract value rose from RM9.128 billion (2014 signing) to RM11.22 billion. Maharaja Lela's first sea-going was on 28 January 2026; first delivery is scheduled December 2026, about 7 years 8 months past the contractual April 2019 date.

cards.big: LCS contract value reached RM11.22 billion. Maharaja Lela had its first sea-going on 28 January 2026; first delivery is scheduled for December 2026.
cards.sub: The original contract was signed July 2014 at RM9.128 billion. The programme was cut from six ships to five.

cards.big: Contract value escalated from the RM9.128 billion 2014 signing to RM11.22 billion. Earlier 2011 figures circulated near RM6 billion before contract.
cards.sub: PAC's 2022 report documented severe mismanagement at Boustead Naval Shipyard before MOF Inc acquired and renamed it Lumut Naval Shipyard in 2024.

cards.big: Several named officers and former Boustead directors have faced MACC investigation. One LCS-linked criminal case had a DNAA in March 2025.
cards.sub: PAC findings preceded several public referrals. The relationship between PAC findings and any specific prosecution is procedural, not causal.

cards.big: The Royal Malaysian Navy has waited ~7 years past the April 2019 contractual delivery date. Singapore commissioned comparable Littoral Mission Vessels years earlier.
cards.sub: The delay created a capability gap relative to commissioned peer-navy assets.

cards.big: The programme is no longer 'first ship years away'. It is 'first sea-going 7+ years past contractual delivery, contract value up ~23%'. Accountability tracks remain open.
RM11.22 billion, first sea-going 7+ years past the April 2019 contractual delivery: not total failure, but a procurement system delivering at substantial cost overrun.
